meta.title: 40 Questions Before Easter
meta.sub: One honest question a day — from “does God exist?” to “did Jesus rise?” — building, over 40 days, to the morning everything turns on. Every answer is short, fully cited, and yours to keep. Walk it alone, or bring your whole group.
meta.completeMsg: From “is there a God?” to “He is risen” — you’ve walked the whole case. Now you can give a reason for the hope you have.
meta.shareText: A 40-day journey through the biggest questions about God, Jesus, and the resurrection — one answer a day, building to Easter. Walk it with me?
meta.seasonNote: Perfect for Lent, but you can start any day.
meta.kitLede: This challenge is built for a small group, a family, or a whole church to walk together through Lent. Share the link, or print the full 40-question list to hand out.

movements.t: Is there a God at all?

movements.s: Days 20–25 · The toughest objections, faced honestly
Days 32–40 · The climax, for Holy Week

days.q: Is there any evidence that God exists?
days.why: Start at the foundation: does the evidence point to a Creator at all?

days.q: What caused the universe?
days.why: Everything that begins has a cause. What began the universe?

days.q: Is the universe fine-tuned for life?
days.why: The dials of physics sit on a razor’s edge. Why?

days.q: Where do morality and human rights come from without God?
days.why: If right and wrong are real, where do they get their weight?

days.q: Is consciousness evidence for God?
days.why: You are not just meat. Where does the mind come from?

days.q: If God made everything, who made God?
days.why: The classic comeback — and why it misunderstands the claim.

days.q: Can we trust the New Testament manuscripts?
days.why: Before we ask if it’s true, can we even trust the text?

days.q: Were the Gospels written by eyewitnesses?
days.why: Legend, or the memory of people who were there?

days.q: When were the gospels written?
days.why: Early enough to be checked — or late enough to invent?

days.q: Aren't there contradictions in the Bible?
days.why: The honest look at the hard passages.

days.q: Is there archaeological evidence for the Bible?
days.why: What the spade has turned up.

days.q: Who decided which books are in the Bible?
days.why: Was the canon a power play, or a recognition?

days.q: Did Jesus really exist?
days.why: Even sceptical historians agree on this much.

days.q: Did Jesus actually claim to be God?
days.why: The heart of it: did he actually say it?

days.q: Wasn't Jesus just a good moral teacher?
days.why: The one thing a merely-good teacher can’t be.

days.q: What is the evidence that Jesus is God?
days.why: The case, laid out plainly.

days.q: Did Jesus fulfill Isaiah 53?
days.why: A portrait written centuries before the cross.

days.q: Isn't the Trinity a contradiction?
days.why: One God, three persons — confusion, or coherence?

days.q: Did the church invent Jesus' divinity at the Council of Nicaea?
days.why: What actually happened in 325.

days.q: Isn't Christianity just true because you were raised in it?
days.why: The 'you only believe it because of where you were born' challenge — faced honestly.

days.q: Doesn't science disprove God?
days.why: Has science made God unnecessary? The honest answer.

days.q: Isn't faith just believing without evidence?
days.why: What the Bible actually means by “faith.”

days.q: Isn't God just wishful thinking?
days.why: The projection objection — is faith just a comforting story we tell ourselves?

days.q: Can you be good without believing in God?
days.why: If there's no God, where does goodness come from — and can we be good without him?

days.q: Does naturalism undermine reason?
days.why: If the mind is just atoms in motion, can we trust our own reasoning at all?

days.q: Aren't all religions basically the same?
days.why: Same words, very different claims. Do they really agree?

days.q: What makes Christianity different from other religions?
days.why: The one difference that changes everything.

days.q: Do Muslims and Christians worship the same God?
days.why: Shared vocabulary is not shared belief.

days.q: Was Jesus a Muslim?
days.why: What the Jesus of history actually claimed.

days.q: Did Jesus die on the cross, or does the Quran deny it?
days.why: The one event the earliest evidence will not let go.

days.q: Is Islam true?
days.why: Weighing the claim honestly, on the evidence.

days.q: Is there evidence that Jesus rose from the dead?
days.why: Now the climax. Is there real evidence he rose?

days.q: What is the minimal facts argument for the resurrection?
days.why: The facts even critics grant — and what best explains them.

days.q: Is there any evidence that the tomb was empty?
days.why: Enemy attestation: no one produced a body.

days.q: Did the disciples just hallucinate the risen Jesus?
days.why: Groups don’t share the same hallucination.

days.q: Wasn't the resurrection just a legend that grew over time?
days.why: The creed is too early for legend to grow.

days.q: Why did Paul go from persecuting Christians to dying for Jesus?
days.why: A persecutor turned martyr. What did he see?

days.q: Why did the disciples die for their belief in the resurrection?
days.why: People die for lies they believe — not for what they know is false.

days.q: Why does it matter that women were the first witnesses to the empty tomb?
days.why: The detail no inventor of the era would choose.

days.q: Did Jesus really rise from the dead?
days.why: Easter. Put it all together — and answer the question.
